content_markdown: Gravitational field strength at a point may be defined as

options.label: A
options.text: the force on a small mass placed at the point.

options.label: B
options.text: the force per unit mass on a small mass placed at the point.

options.label: C
options.text: the work done to move unit mass from infinity to the point.

options.label: D
options.text: the work done per unit mass to move a small mass from infinity to the point.

vibe_explanation: Gravitational field strength ($g$) is defined as the gravitational force ($F$) acting per unit mass ($m$) on a small test mass placed at that point, expressed as $g = \frac{F}{m}$. Options C and D describe gravitational potential, not field strength.
